sections.paragraph:
-

(1) to knock at a door, Son 5:2 compare Hithpael.

(2) to drive a flock hard, to overdrive, Gen 33:13. (Arab. دفق to go quickly, pr. to be thrust forward.)

Hithpael, part. מִתְדַּפְּקִים knocking in rivalry at a door (this seems to me to be the signification of the conj. Hithpael in this place), Jud. 19:22 . Hence-
